rationale: The citing paper evaluates JMR methods and domain convergence, while the cited paper is a methodological article on field experiments, so a non-incidental method-related use is plausible. But the evidence here is only one DOI-level match with no in-text citation context, and the citing note does not list this paper among its explicitly central prior works. That makes the pair unsafe to drop but not clearly strong enough to keep outright.
